title: eugenia raran
side_effects: Eugenia raran can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. In some cases, consuming the plant's leaves or bark can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of essential oils. Excessive consumption of Eugenia raran may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue, especially when combined with other medications or health conditions. Furthermore, improper use of the plant can also cause liver and kidney damage in severe cases, emphasizing the need for caution and proper guidance.
